bridge-horizon.description: If set to none bridge horizon will not be used.

bridge-pvid.description: Used to assign port VLAN ID (pvid) for dynamically bridged interface. This property only has an effect when bridge vlan-filtering is set to yes.

comment.description: Short description of the item (string value)

disabled.description: Defines whether an item is ignored or used. By default VPLS interface is disabled.

pw-control-word.description: Enables/disables Control Word usage. Default values for regular and cisco style VPLS tunnels differ. Cisco style by default has control word usage disabled. Read more in the VPLS Control Word article.

pw-l2mtu.description: L2MTU value advertised to a remote peer.

pw-l2mtu.description: L2MTU value advertised to a remote peer.

dst-address.description: Prefix from which route will be generated.

prefix-length.description: Prefix length for generated route (can be specified as integer range). For example dst-address 192.168.0.0/16 and prefix-length 24 will generate /24 routes from 192.168.0.0/16 subnet.

scope.description: Scope to be set, can be set as range

target-scope.description: Target scope to be set, can be set as range

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of properties to return
parameters.name: .proplist

exclude.description: When exclude is set, the interface expects to reject multicast data from the configured sources. When this option is not used, the interfaces will emit source specific join for the configured sources.

groups.description: The multicast group address to be used by the interface, multiple group addresses are supported.

interfaces.description: Name of the interface, multiple interfaces and interface lists are supported.

sources.description: The source address list used by the interface, multiple source addresses are supported. This setting has an effect when IGMPv3 or MLDv2 protocols are active.

exclude.description: When exclude is set, the interface expects to reject multicast data from the configured sources. When this option is not used, the interfaces will emit source specific join for the configured sources.

groups.description: The multicast group address to be used by the interface, multiple group addresses are supported.

interfaces.description: Name of the interface, multiple interfaces and interface lists are supported.

sources.description: The source address list used by the interface, multiple source addresses are supported. This setting has an effect when IGMPv3 or MLDv2 protocols are active.

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of properties to return
parameters.name: .proplist

exclude.description: When exclude is set, the interface expects to reject multicast data from the configured sources. When this option is not used, the interfaces will emit source specific join for the configured sources.

groups.description: The multicast group address to be used by the interface, multiple group addresses are supported.

interfaces.description: Name of the interface, multiple interfaces and interface lists are supported.

sources.description: The source address list used by the interface, multiple source addresses are supported. This setting has an effect when IGMPv3 or MLDv2 protocols are active.

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of properties to return
parameters.name: .proplist

id.description: Parameter to explicitly set the Router ID. If ID is not explicitly specified, then it can be elected from one of the configured IP addresses on the router. See parameters select-dynamic-id and select-from-vrf. (A.B.C.D    (IP address))

select-dynamic-id.description: States what IP addresses to use for the ID election:any - any address found on the router can be elected as the Router ID.lowest - pick the lowest IP address.only-active - pick an ID only from active IP addresses.only-loopback - pick an ID only from loopback addresses (loopback address is considered any non point to point /32 address).only-vrf - pick an ID only from selected VRF. Works with select-from-vrf property. (any|only-static|only-loopback|only-vrf|only-active|lowest[,SelectDynamicId*])

select-from-vrf.description: VRF from which to select IP addresses for the ID election.

id.description: Parameter to explicitly set the Router ID. If ID is not explicitly specified, then it can be elected from one of the configured IP addresses on the router. See parameters select-dynamic-id and select-from-vrf. (A.B.C.D    (IP address))

select-dynamic-id.description: States what IP addresses to use for the ID election:any - any address found on the router can be elected as the Router ID.lowest - pick the lowest IP address.only-active - pick an ID only from active IP addresses.only-loopback - pick an ID only from loopback addresses (loopback address is considered any non point to point /32 address).only-vrf - pick an ID only from selected VRF. Works with select-from-vrf property. (any|only-static|only-loopback|only-vrf|only-active|lowest[,SelectDynamicId*])

select-from-vrf.description: VRF from which to select IP addresses for the ID election.

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of properties to return
parameters.name: .proplist

id.description: Parameter to explicitly set the Router ID. If ID is not explicitly specified, then it can be elected from one of the configured IP addresses on the router. See parameters select-dynamic-id and select-from-vrf. (A.B.C.D    (IP address))

select-dynamic-id.description: States what IP addresses to use for the ID election:any - any address found on the router can be elected as the Router ID.lowest - pick the lowest IP address.only-active - pick an ID only from active IP addresses.only-loopback - pick an ID only from loopback addresses (loopback address is considered any non point to point /32 address).only-vrf - pick an ID only from selected VRF. Works with select-from-vrf property. (any|only-static|only-loopback|only-vrf|only-active|lowest[,SelectDynamicId*])

select-from-vrf.description: VRF from which to select IP addresses for the ID election.

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of properties to return
parameters.name: .proplist

query-interval.description: How often to send out IGMP Query messages over downstream interfaces. (1s..1h    (time interval))

query-response-interval.description: How long to wait for responses to an IGMP Query message. (1s..1h    (time interval))

quick-leave.description: Specifies action on IGMP Leave message. If quick-leave is on, then an IGMP Leave message is sent upstream as soon as a leave message is received from the first client on the downstream interface. Use yes only in case there is only one subscriber behind the proxy.

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of properties to return
parameters.name: .proplist

query-interval.description: How often to send out IGMP Query messages over downstream interfaces. (1s..1h    (time interval))

query-response-interval.description: How long to wait for responses to an IGMP Query message. (1s..1h    (time interval))

quick-leave.description: Specifies action on IGMP Leave message. If quick-leave is on, then an IGMP Leave message is sent upstream as soon as a leave message is received from the first client on the downstream interface. Use yes only in case there is only one subscriber behind the proxy.

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of properties to return
parameters.name: .proplist

expire-interval.description: Time interval [s] polled data is considered valid in the absence of a valid subsequent update from the validator.

group.description: Name of the group a database is assigned to.

preference.description: If there are multiple RTR sources, the preference number indicates a more preferred one. A higher number is preferred.If preference is not configured then lowest remote IP within a group is preferred, if IPs are equal then lowest remote port is preferred.

refresh-interval.description: Time interval [s] to poll the newest data from the validator.

retry-interval.description: Time Interval [s] to retry after the failed data poll from the validator.

vrf.description: Name of the VRF table used to bind the connection to.

preference.description: If there are multiple RTR sources, the preference number indicates a more preferred one. A higher number is preferred.If preference is not configured then lowest remote IP within a group is preferred, if IPs are equal then lowest remote port is preferred.

refresh-interval.description: Time interval [s] to poll the newest data from the validator.

retry-interval.description: Time Interval [s] to retry after the failed data poll from the validator.

vrf.description: Name of the VRF table used to bind the connection to.

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of properties to return
parameters.name: .proplist

action.description: An action to take on the matching packet:drop - silently drop the packet.lookup - perform a lookup in routing tables.lookup-only-in-table - perform lookup only in the specified routing table (see table parameter).unreachable - generate ICMP unreachable message and send it back to the source.

disabled.description: The disabled rule is not used.

dst-address.description: The destination address of the packet to match.

min-prefix.description: Routes from the routing table with specified prefix length is hidden to packets processed by routing rule.Equivalent to Linux IP rule suppress_prefixlength . For example to suppress the default route in the routing decision set the value to 0.

src-address.description: The source address of the packet to match.

table.description: Name of the routing table to use for lookup.

action.description: An action to take on the matching packet:drop - silently drop the packet.lookup - perform a lookup in routing tables.lookup-only-in-table - perform lookup only in the specified routing table (see table parameter).unreachable - generate ICMP unreachable message and send it back to the source.

disabled.description: The disabled rule is not used.

dst-address.description: The destination address of the packet to match.

min-prefix.description: Routes from the routing table with specified prefix length is hidden to packets processed by routing rule.Equivalent to Linux IP rule suppress_prefixlength . For example to suppress the default route in the routing decision set the value to 0.

src-address.description: The source address of the packet to match.

table.description: Name of the routing table to use for lookup.

disabled.description: The disabled rule is not used.

dst-address.description: The destination address of the packet to match.

min-prefix.description: Routes from the routing table with specified prefix length is hidden to packets processed by routing rule.Equivalent to Linux IP rule suppress_prefixlength . For example to suppress the default route in the routing decision set the value to 0.

src-address.description: The source address of the packet to match.

table.description: Name of the routing table to use for lookup.

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of properties to return
parameters.name: .proplist

single-process.description: When enabled all routing related processes are combined into single routing process to decrease RAM usage. When disabled all routing related processes are processed separately, which can improve performance and stability in certain setups.By default, single-process is enabled only for devices with 64 MB of RAM.A reboot is required for this change to take effect.
